title: Risk-off open to the week — tech leads the pullback
tag: SPY, QQQ, DIA, and IWM all closed lower; VIX jumped as volatility returned to start the week.

spy.read: Pulled back from Friday's high, still well above the 20-day

qqq.read: Led the downside — mega-cap tech gave back the most

dia.read: Held up best of the four — value less exposed to the tech pullback

iwm.read: Small caps softer, in line with the broader risk-off tone

sentiment.label: RISK-OFF
sentiment.qualifier: broad pullback led by tech, no single catalyst

weather.volatility: VIX 17.16, up 14.17% on the day — volatility returned after a quiet Friday
weather.tone: Defensive — a day to hold discipline, not chase the dip

cluster: Leadership lane: DIA held up best; QQQ led the pullback — value over growth today
Key catalyst today: none scheduled — no major earnings reported
Secondary catalyst: bank earnings season opens tomorrow (7/14) with JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C all reporting
Small cap vs large cap: IWM (-0.85%) roughly in line with SPY, no clean divergence
Broad participation: broad-based red across all four majors
Rotation note: money moving out of mega-cap tech, not yet clear where it's landing
Behavior rule: one red day after a quiet stretch is not a trend — don't overreact
account: Mission for today: observe only — a single risk-off day doesn't change the plan
Broker status: IB sandbox scoped, not yet connected; Schwab pending
Elevated event risk: major bank earnings (JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C) tomorrow morning — carries into tomorrow's open
checklist: Data flow, broker connections, overnight positions, stop logic: monitoring data-only — no live broker connection yet
Core universe glance: DIA clearest; QQQ cloudiest (led the pullback)
Day-specific catalyst note #1: no major earnings reported today
Day-specific catalyst note #2: big bank Q2 earnings season opens tomorrow morning (JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C)
body_muted: A red day to start the week — nothing that changes the picture on its own.
